name: Sabadell, Espai Rural de Gallecs, Caldes de Montbui — circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Sabadell offers diverse terrain, benefiting from its proximity to significant natural parks. The region features varied landscapes, including the mountainous areas of Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park and Serra de Collserola Natural Park. Green belts like Rodal de Sabadell and paths along the Ripoll River provide accessible routes connecting urban areas with the countryside. This combination creates a network of trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Sabadell The most popular mountain bike trail is Sabadell, Espai Rural de Gallecs, Caldes de Montbui — circular, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Espai Rural de Gallecs and connects to Caldes de Montbui. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Can Magí Spring – Port of Badalona loop from la Creu Alta, a moderate 31.4 miles (50.5 km) path. This route offers a mix of natural and urban landscapes, extending towards the coast. Local mountain bikers also love the Pi de les Tres Branques – View of La Mola loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord, a 23.4 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through areas with views of La Mola, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Mountain biking around Sabadell is defined by natural park mountains, green belts, and river paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy routes to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes. The routes in Sabadell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sabadell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Sabadell?
A: Sabadell offers a wide range of mountain biking opportunities, with over 70 trails available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy paths to challenging, difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Sabadell?
A: The terrain around Sabadell is quite diverse, benefiting from its proximity to natural parks like Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac and Serra de Collserola. You can expect a mix of landscapes, including mountainous areas, green belts, and river paths. Trails feature varied characteristics such as 'roller coaster' sections, narrow paths through vegetation, and technical sections with rock gardens. Some routes involve significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Sabadell?
A: Yes, Sabadell has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Out of the over 70 available routes, 8 are specifically rated as easy, offering a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking near Sabadell?
A: The region boasts several natural and historical landmarks. You can encounter features like the impressive massif and the Romanesque Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, perched on La Mola, offering panoramic views. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt;, various lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Borrell Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic Parc Fluvial del Ripoll with its old mills and green areas.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Sabadell?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Sabadell are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931804611/sabadell-espai-rural-de-gallecs-caldes-de-montbui-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabadell, Espai Rural de Gallecs, Caldes de Montbui — circular&lt;/a&gt; route, which explores the Espai Rural de Gallecs.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sabadell?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sabadell are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 out of 5 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 5,000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the network of trails suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Sabadell offers 36 difficult mountain bike trails. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and technical sections. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39533293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Ases (860 m) – Sant Llorenç Savall loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that offer views of La Mola?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of La Mola, the highest peak in Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31191622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi de les Tres Branques – View of La Mola loop from Sabadell-Parc Nord&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through areas with excellent vantage points of this iconic landmark.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that connect to coastal areas?
A: Yes, some routes extend towards the coast, offering a mix of natural and urban landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32487685" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Magí Spring – Port of Badalona loop from la Creu Alta&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate path that takes you towards the Port of Badalona.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sabadell?
A: Sabadell generally offers good conditions for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While winter can be cooler, many trails remain accessible. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred during this season.

Q: Are there any specific areas known for their rural landscapes for mountain biking?
A: Yes, the Espai Rural de Gallecs is a notable area for its rural landscapes, offering a peaceful setting for mountain biking. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931804611/sabadell-espai-rural-de-gallecs-caldes-de-montbui-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabadell, Espai Rural de Gallecs, Caldes de Montbui — circular&lt;/a&gt; allow you to explore this charming countryside.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, the region's trails often pass by or offer views of historical sites. For example, the Romanesque Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt is a significant historical landmark located within the natural park, accessible via various routes. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Medir Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; on some trails.
tips:
Sentmenat Castle is a castle in the municipality of Sentmenat (Barcelona) declared a cultural asset of national interest.

    The castle is in a strategic place. The façade, oriented to the west, is on the margin of the ravine that forms the Sentmenat stream. Built on a flat ridge of the ridge, 1,300 m northwest of the parish church of the village of Sentmenat.

   It is a large Gothic building rebuilt on older foundations. In the fortress, it is reached through a stone bridge that protrudes from the castle and that seems to replace the old drawbridge. The grandeur of its fortress is manifested with walls three meters thick. The castle is made up of two rectangular bodies, arranged octagonally, with the ends joined by another body, the plan of which describes an arc of a quarter circle, more or less regular. These three bodies surround a patio in the shape of a trapezoid.

Santa Maria de Caldes de Montbui is the parish church of this municipality, in the Vallés Oriental. It is in the center of the urban area, at the southern end of the historic center, right where the Portal de Barcelona used to be. In front of the main door of the church the Plaza de la Iglesia opens, on the north side the Calle de Roma and the Plaza del Olmo. On the west and southeast side, the church overlooks a small square above the timba of the Caldes stream.

    The current church of Santa Maria occupies the place where, until the end of the Middle Ages, the Royal Palace stood.

Gallecs is a rural area just fifteen kilometers north of Barcelona. The main activity of the space is agriculture, and now the majority is organic farming. It is a very good space for leisure and sports. It is a green lung for the area!
Wooden bridge with very original rounded metal railings, ideal for crossing the Ripoll river from the industrial estate to the Bassa de Sant Oleguer.
Also called Pont del Camí de Torre Romeu

waypoints.name: Wooden bridge over the Ripoll River in Sabadell
